quiz.question: She refused to see any job as menial — every role had its dignity. What does 'menial' mean?
options: Low-status and requiring little skill — of a humble, servile nature
Poorly paid in a way that reflected a failure of the labour market, as the evidence showed
Repetitive in a way that offered no opportunity for advancement
Beneath the level of ambition she had set for herself
quiz.answer: Low-status and requiring little skill — of a humble, servile nature
quiz.explanation:
Menial means low-status and servile — requiring little skill and offering little dignity.
• menial = of humble, degrading, or servile character
• From Old French: 'meinie' — a household
• 'Menial tasks', 'menial work' — low-status, unskilled service

quiz.question: The priest assured her that her transgression was only a venial sin, not a mortal one. What does 'venial' mean?
options: Pardonable — minor and not seriously wrong
Forgiven automatically without the need for confession
Insufficient in seriousness to affect her standing in the church
Less serious than she had feared when she came to confession
quiz.answer: Pardonable — minor and not seriously wrong
quiz.explanation:
Venial means pardonable — a minor fault that can be forgiven.
• venial = minor and forgivable; not seriously wrong
• From Latin: 'venia' — forgiveness
• 'Venial sin' (minor) contrasts with 'mortal sin' (grave) in Catholic teaching

quiz.question: The art fair is a biennial event held every other year. What does 'biennial' mean?
options: Occurring every two years
Held twice a year in different host cities
Running for two years before rotating to a new location
Organised every year but alternating between two venues
quiz.answer: Occurring every two years
quiz.explanation:
Biennial means happening every two years.
• biennial = occurring every two years; also: a plant that lives two years
• From Latin: 'bi-' (two) + 'annus' (year)
• Note: 'biannual' = twice a year; 'biennial' = every two years

quiz.question: Perennial plants return year after year without needing to be replanted. What does 'perennial' mean?
options: Lasting or occurring repeatedly over many years
Hardy enough to survive winter without protection
Requiring little maintenance once established
Returning each spring from the same root system
quiz.answer: Lasting or occurring repeatedly over many years
quiz.explanation:
Perennial means lasting through years — recurring or enduring indefinitely.
• perennial = lasting many years; recurring
• From Latin: 'per-' (through) + 'annus' (year)
• 'Perennial problem', 'perennial plant' — coming back year after year

quiz.question: The kangaroo is a marsupial that carries its young in a pouch. What is a marsupial?
options: A mammal that carries its young in an external pouch
A warm-blooded animal native to Australia and nearby regions
Any mammal whose young are born at an early stage of development
A mammal that uses a specialised body part to nurse its offspring
quiz.answer: A mammal that carries its young in an external pouch
quiz.explanation:
A marsupial is a mammal with a pouch — where undeveloped young complete their development.
• marsupial = a mammal of the order Marsupialia
• From Latin: 'marsupium' — a pouch
• Kangaroos, wallabies, koalas, and opossums are marsupials

quiz.question: The insurance company used actuarial tables to calculate the probability of various risks. What does 'actuarial' mean?
options: Relating to the statistical analysis of risk for insurance purposes
Produced by mathematicians working in the financial sector
Used to determine the premiums charged to different customers, as the evidence showed
Based on historical data about accidents and mortality rates
quiz.answer: Relating to the statistical analysis of risk for insurance purposes
quiz.explanation:
Actuarial means relating to actuarial science — the statistical analysis of financial risk.
• actuarial = of or relating to actuaries; concerning risk statistics
• From Latin: 'actuarius' — a record keeper
• 'Actuarial table', 'actuarial calculation' — statistical risk assessment

quiz.question: The aerial view from the helicopter revealed the full scale of the flooding. What does 'aerial' mean?
options: From the air — relating to or occurring in the air
Taken from a distance great enough to show the whole area
Captured using specialist photography equipment
Available only to those with access to an aircraft
quiz.answer: From the air — relating to or occurring in the air
quiz.explanation:
Aerial means of or from the air — relating to the atmosphere.
• aerial = of or in the air; relating to aircraft
• From Latin: 'aerius' — of the air
• 'Aerial photograph', 'aerial view' — taken from the air

quiz.question: The imperial palace was the residence of the emperor and the centre of government. What does 'imperial' mean?
options: Relating to an empire or its ruler
Large enough to house the court and all its functions
Designed to communicate the power of the state
Reserved for the head of state and their immediate family
quiz.answer: Relating to an empire or its ruler
quiz.explanation:
Imperial means of an empire or emperor — relating to supreme authority.
• imperial = relating to an empire or emperor
• From Latin: 'imperialis' — of the emperor
• 'Imperial decree', 'imperial palace', 'imperial ambitions' — of empire

quiz.question: The professor delivered the lecture in a magisterial tone that brooked no disagreement. What does 'magisterial' mean?
options: Having or showing great authority — authoritative and commanding
More formal than the subject matter strictly required
Impressive in a way that prevented the audience from asking questions
Associated with a long academic career and unquestionable expertise
quiz.answer: Having or showing great authority — authoritative and commanding
quiz.explanation:
Magisterial means masterfully authoritative — having or showing commanding authority.
• magisterial = authoritative; relating to a magistrate
• From Latin: 'magister' — a master
• 'A magisterial work', 'magisterial tone' — commanding authority

quiz.question: The town's war memorial is the focus of the annual commemoration each November. What is a memorial?
options: Something established to preserve the memory of a person or event
A monument built to honour those who died in military service, as the evidence showed
A structure around which a community gathers to remember
A public monument that plays a role in civic identity
quiz.answer: Something established to preserve the memory of a person or event
quiz.explanation:
A memorial is something that preserves memory — a monument to the past.
• memorial = something that preserves the memory of a person or event
• From Latin: 'memorialis' — of memory
• 'War memorial', 'memorial service' — preserving memory

quiz.question: Fishermen have used this harbour since time immemorial. What does 'immemorial' mean?
options: So ancient that the beginning is beyond living memory
Long enough ago to predate the oldest written records
So far in the past that no one could remember it starting
Older than any other tradition associated with the area
quiz.answer: So ancient that the beginning is beyond living memory
quiz.explanation:
Immemorial means beyond memory — so ancient that its beginning is unknown.
• immemorial = too long ago to be remembered or traced
• From Latin: 'in-' + 'memorialis' (of memory)
• 'Time immemorial', 'from time immemorial' — since before memory

quiz.question: The barber prided himself on his mastery of the tonsorial arts. What does 'tonsorial' mean?
options: Relating to barbering or hairdressing
Practised only by those who had served a formal apprenticeship
Associated with the traditional craft of the barber's trade
Requiring the precision of a skilled craftsperson
quiz.answer: Relating to barbering or hairdressing
quiz.explanation:
Tonsorial means relating to the barbering of hair — to haircutting.
• tonsorial = relating to a barber's work
• From Latin: 'tondre' — to shear
• 'Tonsorial parlour' = a barbershop; often used humorously

quiz.question: The piscatorial village had lived by the sea for generations, its economy built entirely on fishing. What does 'piscatorial' mean?
options: Relating to fishing or fishermen
Located close enough to the sea to make fishing practical
Dependent on the season for the prosperity of its economy
Known throughout the region for the quality of its fish market
quiz.answer: Relating to fishing or fishermen
quiz.explanation:
Piscatorial means relating to fishing — of or pertaining to fish and fishermen.
• piscatorial = relating to fishing or fishermen
• From Latin: 'piscator' — a fisherman
• 'Piscatorial village', 'piscatorial pursuits' — fishing-related

quiz.question: The archive held a rich pictorial record of life in the city during the 1930s. What does 'pictorial' mean?
options: Consisting of or illustrated by pictures
Assembled from photographs taken by professional photographers
More accessible to general audiences than written records
Covering a period for which no documentary evidence survived
quiz.answer: Consisting of or illustrated by pictures
quiz.explanation:
Pictorial means consisting of pictures — illustrated or represented visually.
• pictorial = of, relating to, or expressed in pictures
• From Latin: 'pictura' — a painting
• 'Pictorial record', 'pictorial magazine' — consisting of images

quiz.question: He was known for his sartorial elegance — always impeccably dressed. What does 'sartorial' mean?
options: Relating to dress and clothing — of or concerning tailoring
Associated with the kind of care that only expensive clothes could achieve
Reflecting a level of investment in appearance unusual in his profession
Recognised by colleagues who had fewer resources to spend on dress
quiz.answer: Relating to dress and clothing — of or concerning tailoring
quiz.explanation:
Sartorial means relating to clothing and tailoring — of dress and its quality.
• sartorial = relating to tailoring or dress
• From Latin: 'sartor' — a tailor
• 'Sartorial elegance', 'sartorial choices' — relating to how one dresses

quiz.question: Her reportorial style favoured plain facts over editorial opinion. What does 'reportorial' mean?
options: Relating to reporting — in the manner of a reporter
Factual in a way that distinguished journalism from commentary
Associated with the standards of a particular news organisation
Trained in the tradition of objective news coverage
quiz.answer: Relating to reporting — in the manner of a reporter
quiz.explanation:
Reportorial means in the manner of a reporter — relating to journalistic reporting.
• reportorial = of or relating to reporters or reporting
• From 'reporter' + '-ial'
• 'Reportorial instincts', 'reportorial style' — characteristic of a news reporter

quiz.question: Earthworms are adapted to their terrestrial habitat — they rarely venture above ground. What does 'terrestrial' mean?
options: Of or living on the land — relating to the earth
Found only in soil-rich environments with high moisture levels
Active mainly during conditions of darkness or dampness
Adapted to a range of conditions across different climates
quiz.answer: Of or living on the land — relating to the earth
quiz.explanation:
Terrestrial means of the land — existing on or relating to the earth.
• terrestrial = of the earth; living on land
• From Latin: 'terra' — earth
• 'Terrestrial animal', 'terrestrial television' — of the land or earth

quiz.question: Archaeologists discovered a burial site dating back four thousand years. What is a burial site?
options: A place where human remains have been interred
A location where ancient people laid their dead to rest
An archaeological site distinguished by its funerary contents
A place of cultural significance to the people who created it
quiz.answer: A place where human remains have been interred
quiz.explanation:
A burial site is a place where bodies have been interred — a grave or cemetery.
• burial = the act of burying a dead body
• From Old English: 'byrgan' — to bury
• 'Burial site', 'burial mound', 'burial rites' — relating to interment of the dead

quiz.question: Her mercurial temperament made her exciting to work with but difficult to plan around. What does 'mercurial' mean?
options: Subject to sudden changes of mood — volatile and quick
Creative in a way that was inseparable from her unpredictability
Exciting precisely because nobody knew what she would do next
Too talented to be managed in the way ordinary employees could be
quiz.answer: Subject to sudden changes of mood — volatile and quick
quiz.explanation:
Mercurial means subject to rapid mood changes — volatile and quick like Mercury.
• mercurial = subject to sudden changes; volatile; lively
• From Mercury — the swift messenger god and the quick-silver metal
• 'Mercurial temperament', 'mercurial personality' — rapid and unpredictable

quiz.question: The kitchen was filled with an ambrosial aroma of slow-cooked herbs and roasted garlic. What does 'ambrosial' mean?
options: Divinely fragrant — deliciously pleasing to the senses
Rich enough to remind visitors of the quality of the cooking
Distinctive in a way that made it hard to identify all the ingredients
Spreading through the whole house rather than staying in the kitchen
quiz.answer: Divinely fragrant — deliciously pleasing to the senses
quiz.explanation:
Ambrosial means divinely fragrant — worthy of the gods.
• ambrosial = divinely fragrant or delicious
• From Greek: 'ambrosia' — the food of the gods
• 'Ambrosial scent', 'ambrosial taste' — heavenly, divine pleasure

quiz.question: The ambassador lived in a palatial residence at the centre of the capital. What does 'palatial' mean?
options: Resembling a palace — large, magnificent, and lavishly appointed
Large enough to host diplomatic functions at the highest level, as the evidence showed
Maintained to the standard expected of a senior official
More impressive than the private homes of most of its visitors
quiz.answer: Resembling a palace — large, magnificent, and lavishly appointed
quiz.explanation:
Palatial means like a palace — spacious, grand, and magnificently furnished.
• palatial = resembling a palace; impressively spacious
• From Latin: 'palatium' — the Palatine Hill (where Rome's emperors lived)
• 'Palatial home', 'palatial suite' — impressive as a palace

quiz.question: Good spatial awareness is essential for architects and surgeons alike. What does 'spatial' mean?
options: Relating to space — concerning dimensions, distances, and positions
Required for any profession involving three-dimensional thinking, as the evidence showed
Measured through standard cognitive tests
Developed through training and practical experience
quiz.answer: Relating to space — concerning dimensions, distances, and positions
quiz.explanation:
Spatial means relating to space — the physical dimensions of the world.
• spatial = of or relating to space and its dimensions
• From Latin: 'spatium' — space
• 'Spatial awareness', 'spatial reasoning' — understanding of space and dimension

quiz.question: The judge dismissed the case on the grounds that the evidence was insubstantial. What does 'insubstantial' mean?
options: Lacking solidity or strength — too weak to support the claim
Insufficient to meet the standard required for a conviction, as the evidence showed
Unable to withstand the scrutiny of cross-examination
Based on testimony that could not be independently verified
quiz.answer: Lacking solidity or strength — too weak to support the claim
quiz.explanation:
Insubstantial means lacking substance — too weak or flimsy.
• insubstantial = lacking solidity; not real or significant
• From Latin: 'in-' + 'substantia' (substance)
• 'Insubstantial evidence', 'insubstantial meal' — lacking substance

quiz.question: The prosecution's case relied entirely on circumstantial evidence. What does 'circumstantial' mean?
options: Based on inference from circumstances rather than direct proof
Too weak to form the basis of a successful prosecution
Consistent with guilt without directly establishing it
Requiring expert interpretation to be understood by a jury, as the evidence showed
quiz.answer: Based on inference from circumstances rather than direct proof
quiz.explanation:
Circumstantial means based on inference — derived from surrounding facts rather than direct proof.
• circumstantial = based on circumstances; detailed
• From Latin: 'circumstantia' — surrounding conditions
• 'Circumstantial evidence' = no eyewitness, but facts point to a conclusion

quiz.question: She was bound by contract not to disclose confidential information about her former employer. What does 'confidential' mean?
options: Meant to be kept secret — not for general disclosure
Protected by a non-disclosure agreement she had signed
Covered by legal restrictions on what she could say publicly
Sensitive enough to have been classified at the highest level
quiz.answer: Meant to be kept secret — not for general disclosure
quiz.explanation:
Confidential means secret — intended to be kept private and not disclosed.
• confidential = meant to be kept secret
• From Latin: 'confidere' — to trust fully
• 'Confidential document', 'in confidence' — shared only in trust

quiz.question: His survival was described as a providential escape by those who witnessed it. What does 'providential' mean?
options: Happening as if by divine intervention — fortunately timely
So unlikely that those present could not find a rational explanation
Too convenient to be dismissed as a mere coincidence
Attributed by survivors to something beyond normal experience
quiz.answer: Happening as if by divine intervention — fortunately timely
quiz.explanation:
Providential means occurring as if arranged by providence — fortunately timely.
• providential = occurring as if by divine will; opportunely lucky
• From Latin: 'providentia' — foresight
• 'Providential escape', 'providential timing' — fortune seeming like guidance

quiz.question: She made a tangential remark that was interesting but barely relevant to the discussion. What does 'tangential' mean?
options: Diverging from the main subject — only slightly connected
Interesting enough to be worth mentioning despite its irrelevance
Touching the subject briefly before moving in a different direction
Peripheral to the main argument without being entirely unrelated
quiz.answer: Diverging from the main subject — only slightly connected
quiz.explanation:
Tangential means only slightly connected — diverging from the main line.
• tangential = diverging from a subject; only marginally relevant
• From Latin: 'tangere' — to touch
• 'Tangential remark', 'go off on a tangent' — barely touching the main point

quiz.question: He adopted a deferential tone when addressing the senior partner. What does 'deferential' mean?
options: Showing respectful submission — acknowledging another's authority
More formal than he would have used with a colleague of equal rank
Designed to avoid giving offence to someone with more experience
Professional in a way that recognised the hierarchy of the firm
quiz.answer: Showing respectful submission — acknowledging another's authority
quiz.explanation:
Deferential means showing respectful submission — yielding to another's judgment.
• deferential = showing respect and submission to another
• From Latin: 'deferre' — to carry down
• 'Deferential manner', 'deferential to authority' — respectful submission

quiz.question: The novel explored the existential angst of a generation that had lost its certainties. What does 'existential' mean?
options: Relating to the meaning and purpose of human existence
Associated with the philosophy that emerged after the Second World War
Expressed through characters who questioned everything they believed
Relevant to an era in which traditional values had collapsed
quiz.answer: Relating to the meaning and purpose of human existence
quiz.explanation:
Existential means relating to existence — to the meaning and nature of being.
• existential = of or relating to existence; concerning the meaning of life
• From Latin: 'existere' — to exist
• 'Existential crisis', 'existential question' — concerning the nature of existence

quiz.question: The steps must be followed in strict sequential order for the process to work. What does 'sequential' mean?
options: Following in a logical sequence — one after another in order
Listed in the order of importance rather than chronology, as the evidence showed
Documented so carefully that no step could be missed
Arranged to match the physical layout of the equipment
quiz.answer: Following in a logical sequence — one after another in order
quiz.explanation:
Sequential means following in sequence — occurring in an ordered series.
• sequential = following a logical or chronological order
• From Latin: 'sequi' — to follow
• 'Sequential steps', 'sequential order' — following one after another

quiz.question: He spent an hour on an inconsequential detail while the main issue remained unresolved. What does 'inconsequential' mean?
options: Of little or no importance — trivial
Interesting but not relevant to the problem at hand
Too minor to justify the time he was giving it
Unworthy of attention when bigger issues remained
quiz.answer: Of little or no importance — trivial
quiz.explanation:
Inconsequential means trivially unimportant — without significant consequence.
• inconsequential = not important; irrelevant
• From Latin: 'in-' + 'consequentia' (consequence)
• 'Inconsequential detail', 'inconsequential remark' — not worth noting

quiz.question: The couple exchanged nuptial vows in a simple ceremony by the sea. What does 'nuptial' mean?
options: Relating to marriage or weddings
Performed in front of witnesses as required by law
Traditional in a way that had been passed down through generations
Binding from the moment they were spoken
quiz.answer: Relating to marriage or weddings
quiz.explanation:
Nuptial means relating to marriage — of or pertaining to a wedding.
• nuptial = of or relating to marriage or weddings
• From Latin: 'nuptiae' — a wedding
• 'Nuptial ceremony', 'nuptial vows', 'nuptials' — the wedding itself

quiz.question: The government declared martial law after the coup attempt. What does 'martial' mean?
options: Relating to the military — of war and soldiers
Imposed in a crisis when civilian authority has broken down
Authorised by the constitution in emergencies of national security
Enforced by troops rather than by police
quiz.answer: Relating to the military — of war and soldiers
quiz.explanation:
Martial means relating to the military — of war, soldiers, and armed force.
• martial = of or appropriate to war; military
• From Latin: 'Mars' — the god of war
• 'Martial law', 'martial arts', 'court martial' — military in nature

quiz.question: The client made a partial payment and promised to settle the balance next month. What does 'partial' mean?
options: Incomplete — constituting only part of the whole
Sent as a gesture of good faith rather than full settlement
Calculated to reduce the total outstanding by an agreed proportion
Made on account while negotiations about the remainder continued
quiz.answer: Incomplete — constituting only part of the whole
quiz.explanation:
Partial means incomplete — forming only a part rather than the whole.
• partial = not total; incomplete; also: biased in favour of
• From Latin: 'partialis' — of a part
• 'Partial payment', 'partial eclipse', 'partial to sweets' — incomplete or biased

quiz.question: Both sides agreed that they needed an impartial judge to arbitrate the dispute. What does 'impartial' mean?
options: Not favouring either side — unbiased and fair
Without any prior knowledge of the dispute or its parties
Appointed by an authority that both sides agreed to trust
Experienced enough to reach a conclusion that would be respected
quiz.answer: Not favouring either side — unbiased and fair
quiz.explanation:
Impartial means unbiased — treating all sides without favouritism.
• impartial = not favouring one side; unbiased
• From 'im-' + 'partial'
• 'Impartial judge', 'impartial assessment' — fair, without prejudice

quiz.question: The judge described the attack as a bestial act of unprovoked violence. What does 'bestial' mean?
options: Savagely cruel — having the character of a wild beast
Violent to a degree that placed it outside ordinary categories
Motivated by a desire to cause suffering rather than to achieve a goal
Committed without any of the restraint that humanity requires
quiz.answer: Savagely cruel — having the character of a wild beast
quiz.explanation:
Bestial means brutal and savage — having the cruel character of an animal.
• bestial = of or like a beast; savagely cruel
• From Latin: 'bestia' — a beast
• 'Bestial cruelty', 'bestial act' — beneath the level of human conduct

quiz.question: Ancient astronomers imagined the stars as fixed to a celestial sphere surrounding the earth. What does 'celestial' mean?
options: Of the heavens — relating to the sky or outer space
Imagined by ancient cultures as a physical structure in the sky
Used as a conceptual model before the telescope was invented
Central to the cosmological thinking of the ancient world
quiz.answer: Of the heavens — relating to the sky or outer space
quiz.explanation:
Celestial means of the heavens — relating to the sky and outer space.
• celestial = of the sky or outer space; heavenly
• From Latin: 'caelum' — the sky
• 'Celestial body', 'celestial sphere', 'celestial music' — heavenly

quiz.question: The text was written in colloquial speech — the language of everyday conversation. What does 'colloquial' mean?
options: Belonging to everyday informal speech rather than formal writing
Simple enough to be understood by readers of all backgrounds
Characteristic of the way people spoke in a particular region, as the evidence showed
Chosen deliberately to make the text feel accessible
quiz.answer: Belonging to everyday informal speech rather than formal writing
quiz.explanation:
Colloquial means of everyday informal speech — not formal or literary.
• colloquial = of or relating to informal conversation
• From Latin: 'colloquium' — a conversation
• 'Colloquial expression', 'colloquial language' — informal everyday speech

quiz.question: It was a convivial dinner — good food, good wine, and excellent conversation. What does 'convivial' mean?
options: Friendly and lively — conducive to enjoyment and good company
Well-organised in a way that allowed everyone to enjoy themselves
Memorable for the combination of quality and good company
Relaxed enough for conversations that would not have happened in an office
quiz.answer: Friendly and lively — conducive to enjoyment and good company
quiz.explanation:
Convivial means warmly sociable — encouraging festivity and good fellowship.
• convivial = friendly; lively; relating to good company
• From Latin: 'convivium' — a feast
• 'Convivial atmosphere', 'convivial company' — sociable and warm

quiz.question: The valley was shaped by fluvial processes over millions of years. What does 'fluvial' mean?
options: Relating to rivers — produced by or found in rivers
Caused by the movement of large volumes of water over time
Associated with the erosion and deposition typical of river systems
Relevant to geological periods when water levels were much higher
quiz.answer: Relating to rivers — produced by or found in rivers
quiz.explanation:
Fluvial means relating to rivers — of or produced by rivers.
• fluvial = of, relating to, or produced by a river
• From Latin: 'fluvius' — a river
• 'Fluvial deposit', 'fluvial erosion' — processes of rivers

quiz.question: All warm-blooded mammals regulate their own body temperature internally. What does 'warm-blooded' mean?
options: Able to maintain a constant internal body temperature
Using food energy to generate heat regardless of external conditions
Distinguished from reptiles by the ability to remain active in cold
Able to survive in a wider range of climates than cold-blooded animals
quiz.answer: Able to maintain a constant internal body temperature
quiz.explanation:
Warm-blooded means endothermic — maintaining a constant internal temperature.
• warm-blooded = able to maintain a constant body temperature
• 'Warm-blooded', 'endotherm' — maintaining temperature internally
• Mammals and birds are warm-blooded; reptiles are not

quiz.question: He opened with a banal observation about the weather that left the audience unimpressed. What does 'banal' mean?
options: So commonplace as to be boring — lacking originality
Inappropriate for the occasion because of its trivial nature
More suited to a casual conversation than an opening address
Chosen without consideration of what the audience was expecting
quiz.answer: So commonplace as to be boring — lacking originality
quiz.explanation:
Banal means tiresomely ordinary — lacking any originality or freshness.
• banal = so lacking in originality as to be obvious and boring
• From French: 'banal' — common to all
• 'A banal comment', 'banal conversation' — tediously commonplace

quiz.question: The party degenerated into bacchanal revelry that lasted until dawn. What does 'bacchanal' mean?
options: Wildly drunken and riotously festive
Celebratory in a way that went well beyond what was acceptable
More exuberant than the host had intended it to be
Involving a level of drinking that impaired everyone's judgment
quiz.answer: Wildly drunken and riotously festive
quiz.explanation:
Bacchanal means wildly drunken and riotous — like the rites of Bacchus.
• bacchanal = of or relating to Bacchus; riotously drunken
• From Bacchus — the Roman god of wine
• 'Bacchanalian feast', 'bacchanal celebration' — wild, drunken revelry

quiz.question: The country maintained a large arsenal of nuclear weapons as a deterrent. What is an arsenal?
options: A collection of weapons — or a place where they are stored
A military facility for the storage and maintenance of arms
The totality of a country's nuclear and conventional weapons
A facility subject to international inspection under treaty
quiz.answer: A collection of weapons — or a place where they are stored
quiz.explanation:
An arsenal is a stock or store of weapons — or the facility housing them.
• arsenal = a collection of weapons; a place where weapons are stored
• From Arabic: 'dar as-sina'a' — house of manufacture
• 'Arsenal of democracy', 'a vast arsenal' — stockpile of weapons or resources

quiz.question: The investigation exposed a network of venal officials who had accepted bribes for years. What does 'venal' mean?
options: Corrupt — willing to act dishonestly for money
Careless enough about rules to accept favours without question
Lacking the integrity that public office requires
Exposed by an investigation that had lasted several years
quiz.answer: Corrupt — willing to act dishonestly for money
quiz.explanation:
Venal means corrupt — open to bribery; willing to be bought.
• venal = open to bribery; corrupt
• From Latin: 'venalis' — for sale
• 'Venal official', 'venal system' — corruptly for sale

quiz.question: The cardinal rule of medicine is to do no harm. What does 'cardinal' mean?
options: Of primary importance — fundamental and central
The most important principle in a body of professional ethics
Established at the founding of the profession
Recognised without exception across all medical traditions
quiz.answer: Of primary importance — fundamental and central
quiz.explanation:
Cardinal means of the first importance — fundamental and pivotal.
• cardinal = of fundamental importance
• From Latin: 'cardo' — a hinge
• 'Cardinal rule', 'cardinal sin', 'cardinal virtue' — the most important kind

quiz.question: The doctrine of original sin holds that all humans are born in a fallen state. What does 'original' mean here?
options: Existing from the beginning — innate and inherited
Described in the earliest books of the Bible
Attributed to the first humans rather than to individual choice
Fundamental to Christian theology across most denominations
quiz.answer: Existing from the beginning — innate and inherited
quiz.explanation:
Original (in this context) means from the origin — existing from the beginning.
• original = first; earliest; not derived from anything else
• From Latin: 'originalis' — of the beginning
• 'Original sin' = the inherited condition of humanity from the Fall

quiz.question: The gallery held a major exhibition of Aboriginal art from Australia. What does 'aboriginal' mean?
options: Relating to the original indigenous people of a region
Produced by the first known inhabitants of the continent
Recognised internationally for its distinctive visual language
Protected as the cultural heritage of Australia's first peoples
quiz.answer: Relating to the original indigenous people of a region
quiz.explanation:
Aboriginal means of the original inhabitants — the first people of a land.
• aboriginal = of original or earliest known inhabitants
• From Latin: 'ab origine' — from the beginning
• 'Aboriginal Australians', 'aboriginal culture' — the original inhabitants

quiz.question: The scholar's copy was covered in marginal notes that illuminated his thinking. What does 'marginal' mean?
options: Written in the margin — at the edge of the main text
Added as an afterthought rather than as part of the main argument
Less important than the text they accompanied
Included for the benefit of future readers of the document
quiz.answer: Written in the margin — at the edge of the main text
quiz.explanation:
Marginal means in the margin — at the edge, or of minor importance.
• marginal = of or in a margin; minor in importance
• From Latin: 'margo' — a border or edge
• 'Marginal note', 'marginal seat', 'marginal gain' — at the edge

quiz.question: His 1962 paper is considered a seminal work in the field of molecular biology. What does 'seminal' mean?
options: Highly influential — containing or developing the seeds of later ideas
Published at a moment when the field was ready for transformation, as the evidence showed
Cited more than any other paper from the same decade
Establishing the framework that subsequent research built on
quiz.answer: Highly influential — containing or developing the seeds of later ideas
quiz.explanation:
Seminal means highly influential — sowing the seeds of future development.
• seminal = strongly influencing later developments
• From Latin: 'semen' — a seed
• 'Seminal text', 'seminal work' — the seed from which a field grew

quiz.question: The advertisement was accused of containing subliminal messages designed to influence consumers unconsciously. What does 'subliminal' mean?
options: Below the threshold of conscious awareness
Visible to those who knew what to look for in the image
Effective precisely because it was not openly stated
Designed by specialists in psychological advertising
quiz.answer: Below the threshold of conscious awareness
quiz.explanation:
Subliminal means below the threshold of consciousness — affecting without being noticed.
• subliminal = below the threshold of conscious perception
• From Latin: 'sub-' (below) + 'limen' (threshold)
• 'Subliminal advertising', 'subliminal message' — affecting the unconscious mind

quiz.question: The museum charged a nominal fee of £1 — enough to deter the casual visitor while remaining accessible. What does 'nominal' mean?
options: Very small — trivial in comparison to real value
Set low enough to qualify as accessible to most visitors
Intended as a token rather than a significant source of revenue
Calculated to cover administration without generating profit
quiz.answer: Very small — trivial in comparison to real value
quiz.explanation:
Nominal means trivially small — in name only; not reflecting real value.
• nominal = in name only; very small in comparison to real value
• From Latin: 'nomen' — a name
• 'Nominal fee', 'nominal head of state' — name without full reality

quiz.question: She received the news of her terminal illness with extraordinary calm. What does 'terminal' mean?
options: Predicted to cause death — having no cure
Diagnosed at a stage when treatment could no longer help
Progressive in a way that could not be slowed by intervention
Requiring palliative rather than curative care
quiz.answer: Predicted to cause death — having no cure
quiz.explanation:
Terminal means ending in death — describing an illness with no cure.
• terminal = leading inevitably to death; at the end
• From Latin: 'terminus' — an end
• 'Terminal illness', 'terminal station', 'terminal velocity' — the final end

quiz.question: A provisional government was installed to manage the transition to democracy. What does 'provisional' mean?
options: Temporary — serving for the time being until something permanent is established
Appointed in an emergency by those with authority to do so
Lacking the legitimacy of a government chosen by the people
Given a mandate to organise elections rather than to govern long-term, as the evidence showed
quiz.answer: Temporary — serving for the time being until something permanent is established
quiz.explanation:
Provisional means temporary — serving until a permanent arrangement is made.
• provisional = temporary; arranged for the time being
• From Latin: 'provisio' — foresight
• 'Provisional licence', 'provisional government' — temporary arrangement

quiz.question: His unidimensional view of the problem led him to miss its complexity. What does 'unidimensional' mean?
options: Having only one dimension — seeing things in an oversimplified way
Focused on a single cause when multiple factors were at work
Developed without consultation with those most affected
Based on a reading of the evidence that selected only supporting data
quiz.answer: Having only one dimension — seeing things in an oversimplified way
quiz.explanation:
Unidimensional means one-dimensional — lacking the complexity of multiple perspectives.
• unidimensional = having only one dimension; oversimplified
• From Latin: 'uni-' (one) + 'dimensio' (dimension)
• 'Unidimensional analysis' = seeing only one aspect of a complex reality

quiz.question: Economic theory assumes that actors always make rational choices. What does 'rational' mean?
options: Based on reason and logic — not influenced by emotion
Made with full knowledge of all available alternatives
Consistent with the stated preferences of the person making it
Capable of being defended with reference to objective criteria
quiz.answer: Based on reason and logic — not influenced by emotion
quiz.explanation:
Rational means based on reason — logical and not led by emotion.
• rational = based on reason; sensible
• From Latin: 'ratio' — reason
• 'Rational decision', 'rational actor' — guided by logic rather than feeling

quiz.question: The gravitational pull of the larger planet distorted the orbit of the smaller one. What does 'gravitational' mean?
options: Relating to the force of gravity — the attraction between masses
Relevant to any system in which bodies orbit a central mass
Measurable in units that describe the force between two bodies
Describing the interaction between all bodies in the solar system
quiz.answer: Relating to the force of gravity — the attraction between masses
quiz.explanation:
Gravitational means relating to gravity — the force of attraction between masses.
• gravitational = relating to gravitation
• From Latin: 'gravis' — heavy
• 'Gravitational pull', 'gravitational field' — the force of gravity

quiz.question: Investors can now buy fractional shares in expensive stocks. What does 'fractional' mean?
options: Representing a small part of a whole
Designed for investors who cannot afford a full share
Valued at a proportion of the price of the underlying asset
Created by platforms that allow smaller investment amounts
quiz.answer: Representing a small part of a whole
quiz.explanation:
Fractional means constituting a fraction — a small part of a whole.
• fractional = of or relating to a fraction; very small
• From Latin: 'fractio' — a breaking
• 'Fractional share', 'fractional distillation' — a small part of the whole

quiz.question: The court had to decide whether the act was intentional or accidental. What does 'intentional' mean?
options: Done deliberately — not accidental
Planned in advance as opposed to occurring spontaneously
Carried out with knowledge of the likely consequences
Constituting the higher level of culpability under the law
quiz.answer: Done deliberately — not accidental
quiz.explanation:
Intentional means deliberate — done with intention, not by accident.
• intentional = done on purpose; deliberate
• From Latin: 'intentio' — a stretching toward
• 'Intentional foul', 'intentional act' — done on purpose

quiz.question: Conventional wisdom holds that it is best to save a proportion of each pay packet. What does 'conventional' mean?
options: Based on accepted practice or general agreement — widely accepted
Taught in financial courses as the standard recommendation
Repeated so often it had become received wisdom
Supported by most financial advisers as the sensible approach, as the evidence showed
quiz.answer: Based on accepted practice or general agreement — widely accepted
quiz.explanation:
Conventional means following accepted norms — widely agreed upon.
• conventional = based on or conforming to accepted standards
• From Latin: 'conventio' — an agreement
• 'Conventional wisdom', 'conventional weapon' — the standard, accepted kind

quiz.question: The choir sang devotional songs that had been part of the service for centuries. What does 'devotional' mean?
options: Relating to religious worship and prayer
Traditional in a way that connected the congregation to the past
Chosen for their capacity to produce a meditative atmosphere
Reserved for the most solemn moments of the service
quiz.answer: Relating to religious worship and prayer
quiz.explanation:
Devotional means relating to devotion — used in religious worship.
• devotional = of or used in religious worship
• From Latin: 'devotio' — consecration
• 'Devotional music', 'devotional practice' — relating to worship

quiz.question: The tribunal agreed to hear the case as an exceptional measure. What does 'exceptional' mean?
options: Unusual and not covered by the normal rules — extraordinary
Too important to be dismissed through a procedural technicality
Attracting the attention of those responsible for the tribunal
Admitted under a provision designed for unusual circumstances
quiz.answer: Unusual and not covered by the normal rules — extraordinary
quiz.explanation:
Exceptional means unusually out of the ordinary — not covered by normal rules.
• exceptional = forming an exception; unusual; very good
• From Latin: 'exceptio' — an exception
• 'Exceptional circumstances', 'exceptional talent' — outside the normal range

quiz.question: It was an entirely unexceptional day — nothing out of the ordinary happened. What does 'unexceptional' mean?
options: Ordinary — not distinguished in any way
Quiet in a way that provided a welcome contrast to recent weeks
Typical of the kind of day that most working weeks contained
Offering no particular challenges or particularly pleasant moments
quiz.answer: Ordinary — not distinguished in any way
quiz.explanation:
Unexceptional means ordinary — not standing out in any way.
• unexceptional = not out of the ordinary; unremarkable
• Opposite of exceptional
• 'Unexceptional performance', 'an unexceptional day' — undistinguished

quiz.question: The heated seats are available as an optional extra at additional cost. What does 'optional' mean?
options: Available by choice — not compulsory
Offered to customers who want a higher level of comfort
Priced separately because not all buyers would want it
Selected by buyers who are willing to pay a premium
quiz.answer: Available by choice — not compulsory
quiz.explanation:
Optional means not compulsory — available as a choice rather than required.
• optional = left to individual choice; not obligatory
• From Latin: 'optio' — choice
• 'Optional subject', 'optional extra' — you may choose but need not

quiz.question: Freedom of speech is one of the constitutional rights protected in a democracy. What does 'constitutional' mean?
options: Established by and consistent with a country's constitution
Protected by the legal framework governing the state
Enforceable in court when government attempts to restrict them
Recognised by international human rights standards
quiz.answer: Established by and consistent with a country's constitution
quiz.explanation:
Constitutional means established by or consistent with a constitution.
• constitutional = of or relating to a constitution; legally established
• From Latin: 'constitutio' — an established order
• 'Constitutional right', 'unconstitutional act' — relating to the constitution

quiz.question: Schoenberg's atonal music abandoned the traditional sense of a home key. What does 'atonal' mean?
options: Not written in any particular musical key — lacking tonality
Composed using techniques that many listeners found unfamiliar
Developed as a reaction against the Romantic tradition
Associated with the Second Viennese School of composition
quiz.answer: Not written in any particular musical key — lacking tonality
quiz.explanation:
Atonal means without a tonal centre — not written in a key.
• atonal = not written in any key; lacking tonality
• From 'a-' (without) + 'tonal'
• 'Atonal composition', 'atonality' — music without a key centre

quiz.question: She felt a powerful maternal instinct the moment she held the baby. What does 'maternal' mean?
options: Relating to a mother — of or characteristic of motherhood
Felt in the presence of a newborn by many but not all women
Biological rather than learned in its origin
Expressed through behaviours that protected the infant
quiz.answer: Relating to a mother — of or characteristic of motherhood
quiz.explanation:
Maternal means relating to a mother — of or befitting motherhood.
• maternal = of or relating to a mother
• From Latin: 'mater' — mother
• 'Maternal instinct', 'maternal grandmother', 'maternal care' — of the mother

quiz.question: Fraternal twins are born at the same time but develop from different eggs. What does 'fraternal' mean?
options: Relating to brothers — brotherly in relationship
Born together but not genetically identical
Sharing a birthday but not a single origin
Physically similar but not to the degree of identical twins
quiz.answer: Relating to brothers — brotherly in relationship
quiz.explanation:
Fraternal means brotherly — relating to or befitting brothers.
• fraternal = of or relating to brothers; brotherly
• From Latin: 'frater' — brother
• 'Fraternal twins' = non-identical twins; 'fraternal organisation' = brotherhood

quiz.question: The vernal equinox marks the beginning of spring in the northern hemisphere. What does 'vernal' mean?
options: Relating to spring — occurring in the spring season
Associated with the annual transition from winter to summer
Marked by equal hours of daylight and darkness
Celebrated in many cultures as a moment of renewal
quiz.answer: Relating to spring — occurring in the spring season
quiz.explanation:
Vernal means of or relating to spring — occurring in the springtime.
• vernal = of, relating to, or occurring in spring
• From Latin: 'ver' — spring
• 'Vernal equinox', 'vernal bloom' — of the spring

quiz.question: Many animals follow a diurnal cycle — active by day and resting at night. What does 'diurnal' mean?
options: Relating to or occurring during the day
Active during daylight as opposed to nocturnal hours
Following a pattern determined by the position of the sun
Distinguished from nocturnal species by its waking hours
quiz.answer: Relating to or occurring during the day
quiz.explanation:
Diurnal means of or during the day — occurring in daylight hours.
• diurnal = of the day; active during daylight
• From Latin: 'dies' — a day
• 'Diurnal animal' contrasts with 'nocturnal animal' — active in day vs. night

quiz.question: Owls are nocturnal animals that hunt under the cover of darkness. What does 'nocturnal' mean?
options: Active at night — relating to or occurring in the night
Adapted for hunting using senses other than vision
Found in habitats where darkness provides cover from predators
Active during the hours when most competing species are sleeping
quiz.answer: Active at night — relating to or occurring in the night
quiz.explanation:
Nocturnal means active at night — occurring during the hours of darkness.
• nocturnal = of, relating to, or occurring at night
• From Latin: 'nox' — night
• 'Nocturnal creature', 'nocturnal habits' — night-active

quiz.question: The case was referred to an international tribunal for adjudication. What is a tribunal?
options: A court or other judicial body for adjudication
An international institution with authority to resolve disputes
A body constituted under international law to hear cases
A judicial forum established by treaty between states
quiz.answer: A court or other judicial body for adjudication
quiz.explanation:
A tribunal is a body for adjudication — a court or judicial forum.
• tribunal = a court of justice; a judicial body
• From Latin: 'tribunal' — the raised seat of a magistrate
• 'Employment tribunal', 'war crimes tribunal' — judicial body

quiz.question: The commune practised communal living — all resources were shared. What does 'communal' mean?
options: Shared by all members of a community — used together
Organised so that nobody owned more than anyone else
Based on a philosophy that prioritised the group over the individual
Arranged to eliminate the inequality that private ownership created
quiz.answer: Shared by all members of a community — used together
quiz.explanation:
Communal means shared by a community — used or held in common.
• communal = shared by all members of a community
• From Latin: 'communis' — common
• 'Communal garden', 'communal meal' — shared among the group

quiz.question: The pilot navigated carefully through the shoals at the river's mouth. What is a shoal?
options: A shallow area of water — a sandbank or reef near the surface
A stretch of river where the channel narrows and slows
A zone of turbulent water caused by conflicting currents
A hazard marked on navigational charts with warning symbols, as the evidence showed
quiz.answer: A shallow area of water — a sandbank or reef near the surface
quiz.explanation:
A shoal is a shallow stretch of water — a sandbank or area of reduced depth.
• shoal = a shallow area of water; a sandbank; also a large group of fish
• From Old English: 'scald' — shallow
• 'Shoal water', 'navigate shoals' — hazardously shallow

quiz.question: The bank charged interest on the principal amount of the loan. What does 'principal' mean?
options: The main or original sum — excluding interest or additions
The amount borrowed before any payments had been made
The figure on which interest calculations were based
The debt outstanding at the time of the original agreement, as the evidence showed
quiz.answer: The main or original sum — excluding interest or additions
quiz.explanation:
Principal (as a noun) means the original sum — the capital amount, not interest.
• principal = first in importance; the original sum of a loan
• From Latin: 'principalis' — first
• 'Principal and interest', 'principal of a school' — the main one

quiz.question: The cerebral cortex is responsible for higher cognitive functions including language and reasoning. What does 'cerebral' mean?
options: Relating to the brain — of the cerebrum
Associated with the most evolutionarily recent part of the brain
Responsible for functions unique to higher mammals
Located in the upper region of the brain above the brain stem
quiz.answer: Relating to the brain — of the cerebrum
quiz.explanation:
Cerebral means relating to the brain — of or involving the cerebrum.
• cerebral = of or relating to the cerebrum; intellectual
• From Latin: 'cerebrum' — the brain
• 'Cerebral cortex', 'cerebral person' — of the brain or intellect

quiz.question: The Gothic cathedral soared upward with flying buttresses and pointed arches. What does 'Gothic' mean here?
options: Relating to the medieval architectural style featuring pointed arches
Built between the 12th and 16th centuries across Western Europe
Characterised by the engineering solutions that allowed greater height
Designed to produce an atmosphere of awe through verticality
quiz.answer: Relating to the medieval architectural style featuring pointed arches
quiz.explanation:
Gothic (in architecture) means relating to the medieval style of pointed arches and vaulting.
• Gothic = relating to the style of architecture prevalent in medieval Europe
• Named after the Goths — originally a pejorative term later reclaimed
• 'Gothic cathedral', 'Gothic novel' — medieval style or dark atmosphere

quiz.question: The audience had an immediate visceral reaction to the opening scene. What does 'visceral' mean?
options: Felt in the gut — instinctive and deeply felt
Produced by images too intense to process intellectually
Physical in a way that bypassed rational assessment
Strong enough to cause a measurable physiological response
quiz.answer: Felt in the gut — instinctive and deeply felt
quiz.explanation:
Visceral means instinctive and deeply felt — relating to the viscera (internal organs).
• visceral = relating to the internal organs; instinctive and deep-seated
• From Latin: 'viscera' — internal organs
• 'Visceral reaction', 'visceral fear' — felt in the body, not just the mind

quiz.question: The city's feral cats lived in the parks and survived by hunting. What does 'feral' mean?
options: Wild and untamed — having reverted to a wild state
Descended from domestic animals that had been abandoned
Too wild to be safely handled by untrained members of the public
Living independently of humans in an urban environment
quiz.answer: Wild and untamed — having reverted to a wild state
quiz.explanation:
Feral means wild — having escaped from domestication and returned to a wild state.
• feral = in a wild state, especially after escape from captivity
• From Latin: 'ferus' — wild
• 'Feral cat', 'feral child' — once domestic or civilised, now wild

quiz.question: A printer is a peripheral device that connects to the main computer. What does 'peripheral' mean?
options: External and supplementary — not part of the central system
Connected to the main unit through a standard interface
Optional in the sense that the computer can function without it
Designed to extend the functionality of a central processing unit
quiz.answer: External and supplementary — not part of the central system
quiz.explanation:
Peripheral means at the edge — supplementary and not central.
• peripheral = of or relating to the periphery; supplementary
• From Greek: 'periphereia' — a circumference
• 'Peripheral device', 'peripheral vision', 'peripheral concern' — at the edge

quiz.question: Cherry blossom is prized precisely for its ephemeral beauty — it lasts only a week. What does 'ephemeral' mean?
options: Lasting only a very short time — transient
Beautiful in a way that was heightened by the knowledge of its brevity
Seasonal and therefore dependent on conditions that changed rapidly
Produced once a year and therefore all the more precious
quiz.answer: Lasting only a very short time — transient
quiz.explanation:
Ephemeral means short-lived — existing for only a brief time.
• ephemeral = lasting a very short time
• From Greek: 'ephemeros' — lasting only a day
• 'Ephemeral pleasure', 'ephemeral species' — fleeting, transient

quiz.question: Her career was not linear — she made several lateral movements before finding her niche. What does 'lateral' mean?
options: Sideways — moving to a position of similar rather than higher status
A career move that expanded her skills rather than her seniority, as the evidence showed
A transfer to a different department at the same grade
A change of role that positioned her for future promotion
quiz.answer: Sideways — moving to a position of similar rather than higher status
quiz.explanation:
Lateral means sideways — moving to the side rather than up or down.
• lateral = of or to the side
• From Latin: 'latus' — a side
• 'Lateral movement', 'lateral thinking' — sideways, not linear

quiz.question: The country took unilateral action without consulting its allies. What does 'unilateral' mean?
options: Done by one party alone — without agreement from others
Taken before the alliance had been consulted or given approval
Justified by the urgency of the situation but regretted diplomatically
Permitted under international law in certain defined circumstances
quiz.answer: Done by one party alone — without agreement from others
quiz.explanation:
Unilateral means one-sided — done by one party alone without others' agreement.
• unilateral = done by one side only
• From Latin: 'uni-' (one) + 'latus' (side)
• 'Unilateral disarmament', 'unilateral decision' — one party acting alone

quiz.question: She offered her house as collateral against the loan. What is collateral?
options: An asset pledged as security for a loan
A guarantee offered to a bank to reduce its lending risk
The property that would be seized in the event of default
Evidence of assets submitted as part of a loan application
quiz.answer: An asset pledged as security for a loan
quiz.explanation:
Collateral is an asset pledged as security — held against the risk of default.
• collateral = something pledged as security for a loan
• From Latin: 'collateralis' — side by side
• 'Collateral damage', 'collateral for a mortgage' — alongside as security

quiz.question: A literal translation of the poem loses the music of the original language. What does 'literal' mean?
options: Word-for-word — following the exact meaning of each term
Too close to the original to read naturally in the target language
Accurate but at the cost of the poetic qualities of the text
Preferred by those who wanted precision over elegance
quiz.answer: Word-for-word — following the exact meaning of each term
quiz.explanation:
Literal means word-for-word — taking the exact or primary meaning.
• literal = taking words in their exact sense
• From Latin: 'literalis' — of letters
• 'Literal translation', 'literally' — exactly as stated

quiz.question: Community engagement is an integral part of the project's success. What does 'integral' mean?
options: Essential and fundamental — forming a necessary whole
Important enough to warrant its own section in the project plan
Identified as a priority by those who designed the project
Valued because of the goodwill it generated among participants
quiz.answer: Essential and fundamental — forming a necessary whole
quiz.explanation:
Integral means essential — forming a necessary part of a complete whole.
• integral = necessary to make a whole complete; essential
• From Latin: 'integer' — whole
• 'Integral part', 'integral to success' — essential, not optional

quiz.question: Some philosophers argue that natural disasters are amoral — neither right nor wrong. What does 'amoral' mean?
options: Outside the category of morality — not subject to moral judgement
Committed by forces that have no intention and therefore no guilt, as the evidence showed
Beyond the capacity of ethical philosophy to evaluate
Neither praiseworthy nor blameworthy because of its nature
quiz.answer: Outside the category of morality — not subject to moral judgement
quiz.explanation:
Amoral means outside morality — not subject to moral judgement.
• amoral = not involving questions of right or wrong
• From 'a-' (without) + 'moral'
• Distinct from 'immoral' (doing wrong); amoral = beyond morality

quiz.question: Damage to the temporal lobe can affect memory and language. What does 'temporal' mean here?
options: Relating to the temples of the head — at the side of the skull
Located in the region of the brain associated with higher functions
Connecting the frontal lobe to the occipital and parietal areas
Responsible for functions the brain acquired late in evolution
quiz.answer: Relating to the temples of the head — at the side of the skull
quiz.explanation:
Temporal (anatomically) means relating to the temples — the sides of the skull.
• temporal = of the temples; also of time
• From Latin: 'tempora' — the temples
• 'Temporal lobe' = the brain region near the temples

quiz.question: Corporal punishment in schools is now banned in most countries. What does 'corporal' mean?
options: Relating to the body — involving physical punishment
Administered physically rather than through fines or other means
Justified historically by those who believed it deterred misbehaviour
Replaced in most systems by non-physical disciplinary measures
quiz.answer: Relating to the body — involving physical punishment
quiz.explanation:
Corporal means of the body — relating to the physical person.
• corporal = of the body; physical
• From Latin: 'corpus' — body
• 'Corporal punishment', 'corporal' (military rank) — bodily or of the corps

quiz.question: The painting depicted a pastoral scene of shepherds, meadows, and distant hills. What does 'pastoral' mean?
options: Relating to rural life — characteristic of the countryside
Painted in the tradition of classical landscape art
Set in the kind of countryside that no longer existed unchanged
Idealised in a way that appealed to urban viewers
quiz.answer: Relating to rural life — characteristic of the countryside
quiz.explanation:
Pastoral means relating to rural or country life — ideally peaceful and rural.
• pastoral = of the countryside; also: relating to a pastor's care
• From Latin: 'pastor' — a shepherd
• 'Pastoral landscape', 'pastoral care' — rural scenes or priestly care

quiz.question: The littoral zone is rich in biodiversity — the interface between land and sea. What does 'littoral' mean?
options: Relating to the shore — the coastal zone between tides
Subject to daily flooding and exposure as the tide rises and falls
Inhabited by species adapted to both wet and dry conditions
Protected in many countries as an ecologically sensitive zone
quiz.answer: Relating to the shore — the coastal zone between tides
quiz.explanation:
Littoral means of the shore — the coastal zone that experiences tidal change.
• littoral = of or on the shore, especially the tidal zone
• From Latin: 'litus' — a shore
• 'Littoral zone', 'littoral state' — the coastal strip

quiz.question: The cowboys drove the cattle into the corral at the end of the day. What is a corral?
options: An enclosed pen for livestock — especially cattle or horses
A temporary enclosure used during rounding up and sorting
A fenced area where animals are held before being transported
A structure used on ranches to separate different groups of livestock
quiz.answer: An enclosed pen for livestock — especially cattle or horses
quiz.explanation:
A corral is an enclosed pen — used to contain and manage livestock.
• corral = a pen or enclosure for horses or cattle
• From Spanish: 'corral' — a yard
• 'Drive cattle into the corral', 'corral a suspect' — to enclose or round up

quiz.question: Spectral analysis of starlight reveals the chemical composition of a star. What does 'spectral' mean?
options: Relating to the spectrum — especially of light and electromagnetic waves
Used in astronomy to interpret information carried in light
Based on the principle that different elements absorb different wavelengths
Applied to the light emitted or absorbed by a source
quiz.answer: Relating to the spectrum — especially of light and electromagnetic waves
quiz.explanation:
Spectral means relating to a spectrum — the range of wavelengths of light.
• spectral = of or relating to the spectrum; ghost-like
• From Latin: 'spectrum' — an appearance
• 'Spectral analysis', 'spectral figure' — of the spectrum or ghost-like

quiz.question: The ventral fins on a fish are located on the underside of the body. What does 'ventral' mean?
options: Relating to the underside — of or near the belly
Located below the lateral line on the body of a fish
Used primarily for steering rather than propulsion
Paired in most fish species and used for directional control
quiz.answer: Relating to the underside — of or near the belly
quiz.explanation:
Ventral means of the underside — relating to the belly or lower surface.
• ventral = of or relating to the belly; on the lower side
• From Latin: 'venter' — belly
• 'Ventral fin', 'ventral surface' — of the underside

quiz.question: The howling mistral swept down the Rhône valley, bending trees flat. What is a mistral?
options: A cold, violent wind that blows through southern France
A seasonal weather event that affects the Mediterranean coast
A wind strong enough to close roads and damage buildings
A phenomenon caused by cold air descending from the Alps
quiz.answer: A cold, violent wind that blows through southern France
quiz.explanation:
The mistral is a cold, dry wind — blowing from the north through southern France.
• mistral = a cold, strong, dry northerly wind in the Gulf of Lion
• From Old Provençal: 'maestral' — masterly wind
• 'The howling mistral', 'mistral wind' — the fierce northwesterly of southern France

quiz.question: The president delivered her inaugural address to a crowd of thousands. What does 'inaugural' mean?
options: Marking the beginning of something — of a formal start
Delivered to mark the beginning of a new term in office
The first formal speech of a president upon taking the oath
Witnessed by representatives of all branches of government
quiz.answer: Marking the beginning of something — of a formal start
quiz.explanation:
Inaugural means of the inauguration — marking the formal beginning.
• inaugural = marking the beginning; of an inauguration
• From Latin: 'inaugurare' — to take omens before installing
• 'Inaugural lecture', 'inaugural address' — the first formal occasion

quiz.question: The building's exterior was covered with a magnificent mural painting depicting the city's history. What is a mural?
options: A painting executed directly on a wall — large and fixed in place
A commissioned artwork painted on the exterior of a building
A public artwork that told a story through visual imagery
An art form that flourished in Mexico and was later adopted globally
quiz.answer: A painting executed directly on a wall — large and fixed in place
quiz.explanation:
A mural is a painting on a wall — executed directly on or applied to a surface.
• mural = a painting made directly on a wall or ceiling
• From Latin: 'murus' — a wall
• 'Street mural', 'Sistine Chapel mural' — large-scale wall painting

quiz.question: She faced the crisis with a preternatural calm that unnerved those around her. What does 'preternatural' mean?
options: Beyond what is normal or natural — extraordinary
Too controlled to be fully understood by those who observed it
Developed through years of experience managing extreme pressure
More reassuring than alarming to those who witnessed it
quiz.answer: Beyond what is normal or natural — extraordinary
quiz.explanation:
Preternatural means exceeding the natural — beyond what is normally encountered.
• preternatural = beyond the normal course of nature; extraordinary
• From Latin: 'praeter naturam' — beyond nature
• 'Preternatural calm', 'preternatural talent' — beyond what is normal

quiz.question: He had a nasal voice that was immediately recognisable on the radio. What does 'nasal' mean?
options: Produced through the nose — having a resonant quality from the nasal passage
Distinctive in a way that made it impossible to confuse with others, as the evidence showed
Associated with certain regional accents in English
Amplified by the shape of the skull in a way unique to the speaker
quiz.answer: Produced through the nose — having a resonant quality from the nasal passage
quiz.explanation:
Nasal means of or through the nose — resonating in or produced by the nasal cavity.
• nasal = of or relating to the nose; sounding as if through the nose
• From Latin: 'nasus' — nose
• 'Nasal voice', 'nasal consonant' — sounds produced with nasal resonance

quiz.question: The government launched a military reprisal for the attack on its territory. What is a reprisal?
options: An act of retaliation — a return blow for harm received
A military response authorised under the laws of armed conflict
An action designed to deter further attacks by demonstrating resolve
A measured response calibrated to match the original provocation
quiz.answer: An act of retaliation — a return blow for harm received
quiz.explanation:
A reprisal is retaliation — an act done in return for harm received.
• reprisal = an act of retaliation
• From French: 'reprisaille' — a taking back
• 'Military reprisal', 'act of reprisal' — responding in kind to harm done
